to reclassify the completed asset from Construction in Progress (CIP)
to the appropriate Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) account
and to record the corresponding accumulated depreciation as of
December 31, 2024.
ii. henceforth, reclassify completed projects from CIP to PPE even if
labor cost is not yet received by the accounting department, provided
the asset is already in use;
iii. work closely with the Engineering Department to compile all
necessary documentation and report these completed projects as PPE
in the financial statements, ensuring that CIP is not overstated, and
PPE is not understated;
iv. for discontinued projects, evaluate whether the carrying value of any
remaining materials should be written off due to impairment or
reclassified to inventory for use in other projects within the LGU;
and
v. establish a periodic review process for all CIP projects to monitor
progress, reclassification, and to ensure that no completed projects
are overlooked or left unreported.

b) the Municipal Engineer:
i. ensure that all accomplishment reports for completed projects are
prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant in a timely
manner to allow proper classification and eliminate discrepancies
between the actual status of projects and what is reflected in the
books;
ii. document and justify delays or abandonment, and consider dropping
those projects if warranted; and
iii. in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, identify and assess
projects that have been abandoned or discontinued for potential
impairment or write-off. These projects should no longer be
recorded under CIP and their status should be properly reflected in
the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
a) the Municipal Accountant:
i. in coordination with the Inventory Committee, ensure the completion
of the physical count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) as
mandated by Section 124 of the NGAS for LGUs and Section 490 of
the GAAM. This should be prioritized to ensure the integrity of the
property records and prevent the risk of undetected loss,
misstatement, or misuse of government assets; and
ii. in collaboration with the Municipal GSO, ensure that all PPE records
are regularly updated and reconciled with the physical count data,
ensuring the integrity and completeness of financial records.
b) the Inventory Committee aim to complete the physical inventory of all PPE,
ensuring that the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and
Equipment (RPCPPE) is submitted to the Audit Team by January 31 of each
year.
c) the Municipal GSO, in coordination with the inventory committee, adhere to
the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, specifically designed
for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances to enable the LGU to
review and clear the discrepancies in its PPE accounts, ensuring accurate
and complete records; and
d) the Municipal Mayor:
i. consider allocating additional manpower or resources to the GSO
and Inventory Committee such as including temporary staff or
additional personnel specifically assigned to assist in the inventory-
taking process; and
ii. improve departmental efficiency by providing adequate training or
resources for the GSO to handle inventory tasks in line with
regulatory requirements.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a) the Municipal Accountant:

i. assign personnel to oversee the proper disposition of dormant
accounts and ensure adherence to the guidelines prescribed by COA;
ii. conduct a comprehensive review and validation of all receivable
accounts, with a particular focus on identifying those that have been
inactive for ten (10) years or more, and classify them in accordance
with the definitions under COA Circular No. 2023-008;
iii. initiate immediate actions for the disposition of dormant accounts,
including gathering and securing available supporting documents,
tracing debtors or accountable officers where possible, documenting
efforts to locate untraceable or deceased debtors, and recommending
qualified accounts for write-off, supported by complete
documentation;
iv. formally follow up in writing with the Fraud Audit Office (FAO) at
COA Central Office regarding the pending issuance of the credit
notice for the liquidated Intelligence Fund account amounting to
₱1,640,000.00;
v. prepare and submit a formal request for write-off of eligible dormant
receivables to the COA, following the detailed procedures and
documentary requirements outlined in COA Circular Nos. 2023-008
and 2016-005;
vi. develop and implement an internal monitoring system to regularly
track the status and aging of receivable accounts. This should include
the assignment of focal personnel responsible for periodic
reconciliation, verification, and follow-up of outstanding accounts to
prevent similar issues in the future;
vii. implement routine validation procedures, including annual
confirmation of balances with debtors, documentation of collection
attempts and, immediate investigation of long-outstanding accounts to
prevent the accumulation of dormant and uncollectible receivable
accounts;
viii. consider withholding the salary of those employees and officers with
outstanding dormant cash advances and are still employed with the
LGU, otherwise, further administrative actions may be pursued to
enforce compliance; and
ix. disclose all dormant or questionable accounts appropriately in the
Notes to Financial Statements in the absence of timely write-off, in
order to preserve transparency and provide accurate information to
users of financial reports.

b) the Municipal Mayor oversee the timely submission to COA of documentary
requirements for accounts recommended for write-off, including sworn
certifications, justification reports, and evidence of exhausted collection
efforts.

observations.recommendation:
With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management
agreed on the following:

1. For the Municipal General Services Officer:
a. conduct a comprehensive physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment
items of the Municipality following the inventory taking procedures and
guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the One-Time
Cleansing of PPE Accounts and subsequently submit to COA a complete
RPCPPE, in the prescribed format;
b. strictly observe the utilization of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) upon
release of small tangible items and semi-expendable items4 costing below
₱10,000.00 for proper monitoring and accountability;
c. conduct annual separate inventory-taking and report for all furniture,
equipment, books and work animal costing below ₱10,000.00 (semi-
expendable items) and small tangible items.

2. For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. expedite substantiation of recorded “beginning balance-unidentified” PPE
items based on the result of the inventory count as recommended above,
with the aid of other relevant corroborating documents, in accordance with
the applicable guidelines;
b. review the PPE list to identify items needed to be excluded from the PPE
accounts relative to the small tangible items and semi-expendable items;
c. make proper adjustments in the books on identified capitalized small
tangible items and semi-expendable items; and
d. coordinate with the MGSO for the conduct of physical inventory count,
updating of inventory reports/records and reconciliation thereof.

3. For the Local Finance Committee to, henceforth, consider the alignment of the
budget items consistent with the adaption of the change in policy relative to
the recognition of small tangible items and semi-expendable items as an
expense account under the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses and
not part of the budget appropriated for capital outlay expenditures.

agreed on the following:

1. For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. intensify consistent and regular issuance of demand letters to concerned
officers and employees, both active and inactive, copy furnished the COA
Audit Team;
b. in coordination with other responsible departments, and of the Local Chief
Executive, to facilitate exhaustion of all possible remedies to demand
liquidation of all cash advances especially those accountable
officers/employees still connected with the agency and impose sanctions
such as withholding of salaries and filing of appropriate charges, if
warranted, as prescribed in Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular 1997-002;
c. in coordination with the Special Disbursing Officer with long outstanding
cash advances for Confidential Fund granted prior to CY 2015, make
representation with the COA-ICFAU to expedite the issuance of the credit
advice, if warranted; and
d. consider implementation/application of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated
December 19, 2016, pertaining to Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-
off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund
Transfers…, to facilitate cleansing of the accounts which remained long
outstanding or dormant for at least ten (10) years and where
settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained.

2. For the concerned Department Heads and Officers/Employees still connected
with the agency to immediately liquidate all of their outstanding cash advances
by submitting all the required supporting documents related to
payments/purchases made therefrom.

3. For the Local Chief Executive, as the primary responsible official of the LGU,
to spearhead and enforce strict adherence with the prescribed laws, rules and
regulations in the liquidation of cash advances in order to safeguard the funds
of the local government.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management
agreed on the following:

1. For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the source agencies, or seek for assistance with the
provincial government and Department of Interior and Local Government,
as applicable, to facilitate gathering of supporting documents and details
with respect to the fund transfer balances which remained outstanding in
the books for years, in order to determine the proper disposition necessary
to settle the accounts;
b. refund unexpended/excess project funds, other than those agreed to be
reprogrammed, to the respective source agency or otherwise request for use
in other appropriate activities, whichever is applicable, in accordance with
the agreement and terms of grant;
2. For the Local Chief Executive to direct the Heads of the implementing
departments (Municipal Engineering Office, MSWDO, Municipal Agriculture
office, etc.), in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to implement
promptly the corresponding PPAs funded by unutilized grants and ensure the
timely liquidation thereof.

With the above observations, we recommended and the management agreed
on the following:

1. For the Municipal Accounting Office to:
a. henceforth, ensure that any unexpended balances of DRRM-MOOE is
transferred to the Trust Fund at the end of the current year;
b. make certain that the details of LDRRMF is properly and correctly
disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements, particularly in disclosing the
STF by year sourced and correctness of continuing appropriations thereof;
and
c. ensure that all continuing appropriations are presented correctly and
completely in the SCBAA.
2. For the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure timely
reconciliation of reports and consistency of data incorporated in various
LDRRMF reports, in order to foster reliability thereof and enhance its
relevance.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management
agreed for the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to timely recommend for the
realignment of projects that are deemed no longer viable to be implemented.

With the foregoing considerations, we recommended and the Local Chief
Executive and responsible departments agreed to refrain from granting any
bonuses, allowances and benefits to individuals in the absence of sufficient legal
or statutory basis to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances in the future.
Similarly, for the Sangguniang Bayan to make certain that no annual or
supplemental budgets are enacted to fund the grant of awards and incentives to
LGU employees and officers, unless in accordance with applicable laws, rules and
regulations.

2. The Municipal Accountant to prepare bank reconciliation statements on a
monthly basis in accordance with existing guidelines to determine the
accuracy of book and bank balances.
3. The Municipal Treasurer to regularly notify payees of their unclaimed checks
to prevent them from becoming stale. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant
should cancel long outstanding checks and consequently prepare necessary
adjusting entries to rectify cash balances and payable accounts in accordance
with pertinent regulations.
4. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct
reconciliation of the accounts between the GL/SL and Cashbooks on a regular
basis.

We recommended the following:
1. The Municipal Accounting Office to substantiate the recorded balances of
unidentified PPEs.
2. The General Services Office (GSO) to undertake a comprehensive inventory
count of all LGU properties identifying the proper classification, status of
serviceability, accountability and location thereof.
3. The Municipal Accounting Office and GSO to coordinate properly in matters
relating to PPE in order to maintain reconciliation of PPE accounts.

2. The MDRRM Officer to ensure that unexpended balances in the Trust Liability-
DRRMF account are incorporated in the annual LDRRMFIP. Regular review and
update of the LDRRMFIP should be undertaken.
3. The MDRRM Officer take action and monitor the timely and full implementation
of the updated DRRMF projects, programs and activities. Status of
implementation of the projects should also be prepared.
4. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to properly classify the
projects, programs and activities and record in the Accounting and Budget
Reports, respectively.
5. The Municipal Accounting Office to ensure that transfer of unexpended QRF and
MF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund is done accurately as of yearend and that
any unexpended balances not fully utilized after five (5) years are reverted back to
the General Fund.
6. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the
Municipal DRRM Office, to maximize utilization of DRRMF

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant the following:
1. Validate / confirm from the agencies concerned the propriety and collectability of
the receivables, requesting them to immediately cause the settlement of the
outstanding balances.
2. Provide allowance for impairment for receivables which collectability thereof
were impaired. Otherwise, write-off the accounts considered not collectible at all
in accordance with existing guidelines.
3. Henceforth, regularly monitor and assess the status and validity of the receivable
accounts to ensure that these are collected when become due and demandable.

We recommended the following:
1. The Sagguniang Bayan to ensure that only eligible PPAs receive appropriation
under the 20% LDF in accordance with DILG – DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
2. The Municipal Development Council to refrain from recommending ineligible
PPAs to be funded by the 20% LDF and likewise regularly monitor the
implementation of eligible ones in order to ensure that the LDF are optimally
utilized.
3. Utilization report of the 20% LDF to be prepared by the Budget Officer
promptly in accordance with pertinent regulations and in order facilitate the
preparation of the Consolidated Quarterly Report on Government PPAs.
(Status of Implementation by MPDC).
4. The Municipal Budget Officer to prepare SAAOB for continuing years, in
addition to SAAOB of current year appropriations.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should transfer the portion of unobligated/unutilized QRF and MF-MOOE to Trust Liability-DRMM account.
observations.managementAction: Transfer the unutilized portion of current QRF and MF-MOOE to Trust Liability-DRMM account.

observations.recommendation: The management through the payroll clerks should closely monitor the status of the net pay of their employees. Payroll clerks should be instructed to follow the prioritization on authorized deductions so that the monthly take home will not fall below what is prescribed by GAA.
observations.managementAction: The employees are amenable of their net take home pay. The management encouraged their employees to prioritize their needs.
observations.reason: Recommendation is being implemented on a gradual basis.

observations.recommendation: The financial statements and other reports shall be submitted by the management within the prescribed period so that timely review and verification of accounts can be effected.
observations.managementAction: The management promised to adhere to the prescribed period so that timely review and verification of accounts can be effected.
observations.reason: Recommendation is being implemented on a gradual basis.

observations.managementAction: Wrap up the formulation of Revised Local Revenue Code of the LGU.
observations.reason: Still assessing the proposed adjusted rates to be applied to certain tax policies

observations.recommendation: Appoint a DRRM Officer and three (3) staff, in accordance with the guidelines stated in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Appointment of mandatory positions.

observations.managementAction: Provide signboards, posters, tarpaulins, streamers or similar materials showing the Projects, Programs and Activities of the Municipality pursuant to the guidelines stated under COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 20, 2013.

observations.managementAction: Provide storage facilities to store and safeguard documents and records so as to protect them from possible loss or damage.
observations.reason: The existing motorpool will be the proposed archival system for storage room.

observations.recommendation:
a. Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations prescribed in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
b. Expedite liquidation of cash advances by issuing demand letters.
c. Effect refund of cash advances through salary deductions, whenever necessary.
observations.managementAction: Strictly implement the laws as required in the liquidation of cash advances. Further, follow-up of the status of Credit Notices will be made regularly.
observations.reason: The balance of unliquidated cash advance composed mostly of Confidential Fund awaiting Credit Notice.

observations.recommendation: Appropriate finding for the operations of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.
observations.managementAction: AAR Provide storage facilities positions.

observations.recommendation: Issue Policy by the Guidelines in the Hiring of Job Order and Plan on how to achieve the ideal and realistic personnel complement or follow the organizational structure of municipality instead of resorting to Job Order scheme.
observations.managementAction: Set policy regarding the hiring of Job Order.
observations.reason: The management still has yet to set policy/guidelines regarding the hiring of Job Order. The guidelines still has yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Local Government Unit strictly observe the limitation on Personal Services, pursuant to Section 325 (a) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Strictly observe the limitation on Personal Services, pursuant to Section 325 (a) of RA 7160.
observations.reason: The PS still exceeds the prescribed limitation.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant should adopt the perpetual inventory method of accounting for all supplies and materials procured. Also, maintain subsidiary ledger cards for each item of inventory.
The Acting General Services Officer should prepare and maintain updated stock cards, monitor issuances of supplies and materials and render the SSMI to the Accounting Office.
The Municipal Accountant should follow the generally accepted accounting principles on the treatment of real accounts. Advise her to make the necessary adjustments to correct the misstatements.
The Municipal Accountant should transfer the unobligated/unutilized portion of current QRF and MF-MOOE to Trust Liability-DRMM account.

The Municipal Planning and Development Council and the implementing department concerned should maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented on time to ensure that intended beneficiaries can avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

The management through the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor the status of the net pay of their employees. Payroll clerks should be instructed to follow the prioritization on authorized deductions so that the monthly take home will not fall below what is prescribed by GAA.
The financial statements and other reports shall be submitted by the management within the prescribed period so that timely review and verification of accounts can be effected.

Require the Municipal Accountant to review the detailed schedule and analysis of Due from and to Other Funds and prepare the reconciliation accordingly.

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to effect the corrections.
Require the Local Finance Committee to adhere to Section 336 of RA 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991, in the use of appropriated funds and savings.

observations.reason: Prepared tarpaulins for infrastructure projects but none for other programs and activities of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Driver's Trip Tickets be duly accomplished and serially numbered to ensure proper use of government vehicle and consumption of fuel. Likewise, Travels and Fuel consumption (II-2 & II-3) should be prepared for each vehicle consuming gasoline to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer strictly comply with the regulations on the submission of the copies of approved contracts, including its supporting documents within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations prescribed in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Expedite liquidation of cash advances by issuing demand letters.

Effect refund of cash advances through salary deductions, whenever necessary.
observations.reason: The balance of the unliquidated cash advances comprised mostly of Intelligence and Confidential Fund. Awaiting for Credit Notice

observations.recommendation: Issue Policy Guidelines in the Hiring of Job Order and Plan on how to achieve the ideal and realistic personnel complement or follow the organizational structure of municipality instead of resorting to Job Order scheme.

The guidelines provided by Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government and Department of Budget and Management, be followed in pursuing projects funded out of the 20% Local Development Fund.

Strictly implement the policy on the use of personal protective equipment by the garbage collectors. An information campaign should be conducted to instill awareness to them. Create a committee to look into this matter taking into account on the review and evaluation of their performances.
Prepare the correct Annual GAD Plan to achieve maximum results, as required by the Philippine Plan for Gender Responsive Development.
